  4. Clean and disinfect tools and workstation.
  5. Reset the station for the next client.

quizQuestions.question: A client has slight recession in both front corners and asks for a perfectly straight, boxed hairline. What is the best professional response?
options: Cut a straight line higher to create a stronger look
Follow the natural hairline and create the sharpest realistic shape without pushing the corners back
Ignore the recession and cut both corners square at any cost
Refuse to line the front hairline at all
quizQuestions.explanation: A professional lineup improves neatness without creating an artificial line that removes too much natural hair or grows out poorly.

quizQuestions.question: What is the safest reason to stretch the skin before razor detailing?
options: It makes the haircut take less time
It helps the razor glide on a flatter surface and reduces the risk of cuts
It removes more hair with each pass
quizQuestions.explanation: Skin tension creates a flatter working surface so the razor moves more safely and precisely.

quizQuestions.question: While detailing the temples, you notice one temple naturally sits slightly higher. What should you do?
options: Cut the lower temple higher to force both sides to match exactly
Ignore both temples and focus only on the nape
Respect the natural pattern and improve visual balance without unnecessarily raising either side
Round both temples to hide the difference
quizQuestions.explanation: The goal is visual balance, not destruction of the natural hairline. Chasing exact sameness often makes the result worse.

quizQuestions.question: A student creates a jagged front line with the trimmer. What is the most likely cause?
options: Using heavy pressure or dragging the trimmer in one uncontrolled pass
Checking the line in the mirror
quizQuestions.explanation: Jagged edges usually come from poor control, excessive pressure, or trying to create the line in one long pass.

quizQuestions.question: After finishing the lineup, what should happen before the client leaves?
options: Only brush the client off and move to the next service
Remove loose hair, apply product if appropriate, then clean and disinfect tools and workstation
Leave used tools aside until the end of the day
Have the client inspect the cut while the station remains soiled
quizQuestions.explanation: State board standards require proper client cleanup plus cleaning and disinfection of tools and the workstation after service.

instructorNotes: Demonstrate front-view checking, not mirror-only side checking.
Emphasize that razor use is optional and contraindication-dependent.
Correct students who try to create a new hairline instead of cleaning the existing one.
Require named skin tension and short anchor strokes during practical evaluation.
competencyChecks: Demonstrates proper hand sanitation, draping, and workstation preparation
Identifies contraindications before service
Maintains skin tension during edging
Preserves natural hairline unless redesign is intentionally planned
Uses short controlled strokes instead of one long sweep
Checks symmetry from the front view
Selects correct nape shape based on client preference
Avoids razor use on contraindicated skin
Completes post-service cleanup and disinfection properly

lessons.title: Flat Top & Classic Cuts
lessons.objective: Execute a flat top and classic taper haircut.
learningObjectives: Execute a flat top with a level, even surface using a comb and clipper
Cut a classic taper with smooth graduation from short to long
Blend the flat top sides into the taper without visible lines
Identify the correct guard progression for a classic taper
lessons.content:
Classic Barbering Cuts

The Flat Top

The flat top requires a perfectly level surface on top of the head. It is one of the most technically demanding cuts in barbering.

  1. Establish the height on top with a pick and clipper
  2. Use a level comb or flat top comb to guide the cut
  3. Work from front to back, maintaining a flat plane
  4. Fade or taper the sides

The Classic Taper

The taper is the foundation of barbering. Hair gradually decreases in length from top to nape.

  • Start with the longest guard on top
  • Work down with progressively shorter guards
  • Blend each transition smoothly
  • Finish with a clean lineup

Sanitation — Required Before Service

Apply universal precautions before every service.

  1. Wash or sanitize hands.
  2. Pre-clean all tools — remove hair and debris before applying disinfectant.
  3. Apply EPA-registered disinfectant. Use clipper disinfectant spray on blades and maintain full contact time per label.
  4. Discard all single-use items after use. Do not reuse porous items — they cannot be disinfected.
  5. Disinfect workstation and chair after each client.

Stop Conditions

Stop the service immediately if you observe:

  • Open cuts or broken skin on the scalp
  • Signs of scalp infection or inflammation
  • Client reports pain or discomfort
  • Tool malfunction or overheating

Blood Exposure Protocol

  1. Stop service immediately.
  2. Put on gloves before touching the affected area.
  3. Apply antiseptic to the client's skin.
  4. Dispose of all contaminated single-use materials in a sealed bag.
  5. Clean and disinfect blood-contaminated tools with EPA-registered disinfectant.
  6. Double-bag contaminated waste before disposal.
  7. Wash hands thoroughly after removing gloves.
competencyChecks: Executes flat top with level, even surface across the top
Executes classic taper with smooth transitions from top to nape
Follows pre-clean → disinfect → contact time sequence before service
Uses clipper disinfectant spray with correct dwell time on blades
Stops service and follows blood exposure protocol if skin is broken
Discards single-use items immediately after use

lessons.title: Haircutting Checkpoint
lessons.objective: Demonstrate mastery of haircutting techniques.
lessons.content:
Module 4 Review — Haircutting Techniques

Review before taking this checkpoint: head sections and reference points, low/mid/high fade technique, clipper over comb, scissor over comb, lineup and edging, and classic cuts. Score 70% or higher to advance.

quizQuestions.question: A mid fade starts at which reference point?
quizQuestions.explanation: A mid fade starts at the temple area, between the low and high fade.

quizQuestions.question: Which technique produces a softer result than clipper over comb?
quizQuestions.explanation: Scissor over comb produces a softer, more natural finish than clipper over comb.

quizQuestions.question: What is the parietal ridge?
options: The bony protrusion at the back of the skull
The widest part of the head
quizQuestions.explanation: The parietal ridge is the widest part of the head and a key reference for high fades.

quizQuestions.question: Point cutting is used to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Point cutting removes weight from the ends and adds texture and movement.

quizQuestions.question: When performing a lineup, you should never:
options: Cut above the natural temple hairline
quizQuestions.explanation: Cutting above the natural temple hairline creates an unnatural appearance.

quizQuestions.question: The flat top is considered technically demanding because it requires:
options: A perfectly level surface on top
quizQuestions.explanation: Maintaining a perfectly flat, level plane on top of the head requires precision and skill.

lessons.title: Shave Preparation & Hot Towel Service
lessons.objective: Prepare the skin for a professional shave using hot towel and pre-shave products.
lessons.content:
Shave Preparation

Why Preparation Matters

Proper preparation softens the beard, opens the pores, and reduces razor drag — preventing irritation and ingrown hairs.

Hot Towel Application

  1. Soak a clean towel in hot water (test temperature on your wrist)
  2. Wring out excess water
  3. Apply to the face for 2–3 minutes
  4. Remove and apply pre-shave oil or cream

Pre-Shave Products

  • Pre-shave oil — lubricates and protects the skin
  • Shaving cream — creates a protective lather
  • Shaving soap — traditional, requires a brush to lather

quizQuestions.question: How long should a hot towel be applied to the face before shaving?
quizQuestions.explanation: 2–3 minutes softens the beard and opens the pores without overheating the skin.

quizQuestions.question: Why should you test the hot towel temperature on your wrist before applying it to a client?
options: To check if it is dry enough
quizQuestions.explanation: Testing on your own wrist prevents accidental burns — the client's facial skin is sensitive.

quizQuestions.question: What is the primary purpose of pre-shave oil?
quizQuestions.explanation: Pre-shave oil creates a protective layer between the razor and skin, reducing drag and irritation.

quizQuestions.question: Which pre-shave product requires a brush to build lather?
quizQuestions.explanation: Traditional shaving soap must be worked with a brush — it does not lather from direct hand application.

quizQuestions.question: Proper shave preparation primarily reduces which of the following?
quizQuestions.explanation: Softening the beard and opening the pores reduces razor drag and the risk of ingrown hairs.

lessons.title: Straight Razor Shaving Technique
lessons.objective: Execute a professional straight razor shave with correct angle and stroke.
lessons.content:
Straight Razor Shaving

Razor Angle

Hold the razor at a 30-degree angle to the skin. Too steep causes cuts; too flat causes drag.

The Three-Pass Shave

  1. First pass — with the grain (WTG): Follow the direction of hair growth. Removes most of the beard.
  2. Second pass — across the grain (XTG): Cut perpendicular to growth. Closer result.
  3. Third pass — against the grain (ATG): Closest shave. Only for clients with no sensitivity.

Skin Stretching

Use your free hand to keep the skin taut at all times. Loose skin causes nicks and uneven shaving.

Stroke Technique

Use short, controlled strokes. Rinse the blade after every 2–3 strokes. Never drag the razor.

quizQuestions.question: At what angle should a straight razor be held during shaving?
quizQuestions.explanation: 30 degrees provides optimal closeness without increasing the risk of cuts.

quizQuestions.question: What is the direction of the FIRST pass in a three-pass shave?
quizQuestions.explanation: The first pass always goes with the grain (WTG) to remove bulk safely before closer passes.

quizQuestions.question: Why must skin be kept taut during straight razor shaving?
options: Loose skin causes nicks and uneven shaving
quizQuestions.explanation: A taut surface allows the razor to glide smoothly and safely without catching loose skin.

quizQuestions.question: How often should the razor be rinsed during a shave?
options: At the end of each pass only
quizQuestions.explanation: Rinsing every 2–3 strokes removes cut hair and lather so the blade stays effective.

quizQuestions.question: The against-the-grain (ATG) third pass is appropriate only for:
options: Clients who request it regardless of skin condition
quizQuestions.explanation: The ATG pass delivers the closest shave but can irritate sensitive skin — only use when no sensitivity is present.

lessons.title: Beard Design & Shaping
lessons.objective: Design and shape a beard to complement the client's face shape.
lessons.content:
Beard Design

Face Shapes

  • Oval — most beard styles work; maintain natural proportions
  • Round — add length on the chin; keep sides tight
  • Square — round the corners; fuller on the chin
  • Oblong — keep sides full; minimize chin length

Beard Lines

  • Cheek line — natural or defined; never too low
  • Neckline — two finger-widths above the Adam's apple; the most common mistake is setting it too high
  • Mustache line — follow the natural lip line

Trimming Technique

  1. Comb beard downward to its natural fall
  2. Trim to desired length with guards
  3. Define lines with trimmer and razor
  4. Apply beard oil to finish

quizQuestions.question: For a client with a round face, where should beard length be added?
quizQuestions.explanation: Adding length at the chin elongates a round face and creates better proportion.

quizQuestions.question: Where is the standard neckline set when shaping a beard?
options: Two finger-widths above the Adam's apple
quizQuestions.explanation: Two finger-widths above the Adam's apple is the industry standard neckline position.

quizQuestions.question: The most common mistake when setting a beard neckline is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Setting the neckline too high shortens the neck visually and looks unnatural.

quizQuestions.question: What is the first step in the beard trimming technique?
options: Comb beard downward to its natural fall
quizQuestions.explanation: Combing the beard down first reveals its natural fall and helps ensure an even trim.

quizQuestions.question: An oblong face shape benefits from a beard style that:
options: Adds chin length and keeps sides tight
Keeps sides full and minimizes chin length
quizQuestions.explanation: An oblong face is already long — full sides add width while a shorter chin avoids elongating further.

lessons.title: Post-Shave Care & Skin Treatment
lessons.objective: Apply correct post-shave products and handle common skin reactions.
lessons.content:
Post-Shave Care

Cold Towel

Apply a cold towel after shaving to close the pores and soothe the skin. Leave on for 1–2 minutes.

Post-Shave Products

  • Alum block — stops minor bleeding from nicks; antiseptic
  • Witch hazel — tones and soothes the skin
  • Aftershave balm — moisturizes and calms irritation
  • Aftershave splash — antiseptic; can sting on sensitive skin

Handling Nicks

Apply an alum block or styptic pencil directly to the nick. Hold for 10–15 seconds. Never use a tissue — it leaves fibers in the wound.

Razor Bumps (Pseudofolliculitis Barbae)

Common in clients with curly hair. Caused by ingrown hairs. Recommend shaving with the grain only and using a single-blade razor.

quizQuestions.question: What product stops minor bleeding from razor nicks?
quizQuestions.explanation: An alum block is an antiseptic astringent that constricts blood vessels to stop minor bleeding.

quizQuestions.question: How long should an alum block be held on a nick?
quizQuestions.explanation: 10–15 seconds of pressure is sufficient for the alum block to constrict the blood vessel.

quizQuestions.question: Why should a tissue never be used directly on a razor nick?
options: It leaves fibers in the wound
quizQuestions.explanation: Tissue fibers can stick to the wound, causing irritation and potential infection.

quizQuestions.question: A cold towel is applied after shaving to:
options: Close the pores and soothe the skin
quizQuestions.explanation: Cold temperature causes vasoconstriction, closing the pores and calming any post-shave irritation.

quizQuestions.question: Razor bumps (pseudofolliculitis barbae) are most common in clients with:
quizQuestions.explanation: Curly hair tends to curl back into the skin after shaving, causing ingrown hairs and razor bumps.

lessons.title: Mustache Trimming & Styling
lessons.objective: Trim and style a mustache to complement the client's features.
lessons.content:
Mustache Services

Mustache Styles

  • Natural — trimmed to follow the lip line
  • Chevron — full, thick, trimmed straight across
  • Handlebar — long ends styled upward with wax
  • Pencil — thin line above the lip

Trimming Procedure

  1. Comb mustache downward
  2. Trim bulk with scissors or guards
  3. Define the lip line with a trimmer
  4. Clean up the philtrum (area between nose and lip)
  5. Apply mustache wax if styling

quizQuestions.question: Which mustache style features long ends that are styled upward?
quizQuestions.explanation: The handlebar mustache has long ends that are styled upward and outward, often with wax.

quizQuestions.question: The philtrum is the area:
options: Between the nose and upper lip
quizQuestions.explanation: The philtrum is the vertical groove between the nose and the upper lip — it is cleaned up after trimming.

quizQuestions.question: What is the first step in trimming a mustache?
options: Define the lip line with a trimmer
quizQuestions.explanation: Combing the mustache down reveals its natural fall and ensures an even trim of the bulk.

quizQuestions.question: Which mustache style is a thin line just above the upper lip?
quizQuestions.explanation: The pencil mustache is a very thin, precise line directly above the lip.

quizQuestions.question: What product is applied when finishing a handlebar mustache?
quizQuestions.explanation: Mustache wax provides hold and allows shaping of the handlebar's upward curled ends.

lessons.title: Shaving & Beard Checkpoint
lessons.objective: Demonstrate mastery of shaving and beard services.
lessons.content:
Module 5 Review — Shaving & Beard Services

Review before taking this checkpoint: hot towel preparation, straight razor angle and three-pass technique, beard design by face shape, neckline placement, post-shave care, and razor bump prevention. Score 70% or higher to advance.

quizQuestions.question: What angle should the straight razor be held at during shaving?
quizQuestions.explanation: A 30-degree angle provides the optimal balance between closeness and safety.

quizQuestions.question: Where should the neckline be set when shaping a beard?
options: Two finger-widths above the Adam's apple
quizQuestions.explanation: Two finger-widths above the Adam's apple is the standard neckline position.

quizQuestions.question: What is the first pass in a three-pass shave?
quizQuestions.explanation: The first pass always goes with the grain to remove bulk safely.

quizQuestions.question: Which product stops minor bleeding from razor nicks?
quizQuestions.explanation: An alum block is an antiseptic that constricts blood vessels to stop minor bleeding.

quizQuestions.question: Razor bumps are most common in clients with:
quizQuestions.explanation: Curly hair is more likely to curl back into the skin, causing ingrown hairs and razor bumps.

quizQuestions.question: How long should a hot towel be applied before shaving?
quizQuestions.explanation: 2-3 minutes is sufficient to soften the beard and open the pores.

lessons.title: Hair Color Theory
lessons.objective: Explain the color wheel and how it applies to hair color services.
lessons.content:
Hair Color Theory

The Color Wheel

Primary colors: red, yellow, blue. Secondary colors are made by mixing two primaries. Complementary colors cancel each other out — used to neutralize unwanted tones.

Hair Color Levels

Hair color is measured on a scale of 1 (black) to 10 (lightest blonde). Lifting hair requires removing existing pigment with developer.

Types of Hair Color

  • Temporary — coats the cuticle; washes out in 1-2 shampoos
  • Semi-permanent — no developer; lasts 4-6 weeks
  • Demi-permanent — low-volume developer; lasts 6-8 weeks
  • Permanent — opens cuticle with developer; permanent change

quizQuestions.question: What are the three primary colors?
quizQuestions.explanation: Red, yellow, and blue are the three primary colors. All other colors are made by mixing them.

quizQuestions.question: On the hair color level scale, level 1 represents:
quizQuestions.explanation: Level 1 is the darkest (black). Level 10 is the lightest blonde.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of hair color deposits tone WITHOUT lifting the natural color?
quizQuestions.explanation: Semi-permanent color deposits tone without using developer, so it cannot lift the natural color.

quizQuestions.question: Complementary colors are used in hair color to:
options: Neutralize and cancel out unwanted tones
quizQuestions.explanation: Complementary colors sit opposite each other on the color wheel and cancel each other — e.g., violet neutralizes yellow.

quizQuestions.question: Permanent hair color requires a developer because it needs to:
options: Open the cuticle to deposit color
quizQuestions.explanation: Developer (hydrogen peroxide) swells and opens the cuticle so permanent color can enter the cortex.

lessons.title: Chemical Safety & Patch Testing
lessons.objective: Perform a patch test and identify chemical service contraindications.
lessons.content:
Chemical Safety

Patch Test

A patch test must be performed 24-48 hours before any chemical service. Apply a small amount of product behind the ear or inside the elbow. If redness, swelling, or itching occurs — do not proceed.

PPE for Chemical Services

  • Nitrile gloves — always
  • Protective apron
  • Eye protection when mixing
  • Ensure adequate ventilation

Contraindications

Do not perform chemical services on clients with: scalp abrasions, recent chemical services, known allergies to ingredients, or compromised scalp health.

quizQuestions.question: When must a patch test be performed before a chemical service?
quizQuestions.explanation: A patch test needs 24–48 hours to reveal any allergic reaction before the full service.

quizQuestions.question: What PPE is ALWAYS required when performing chemical services?
quizQuestions.explanation: Nitrile gloves are always required to protect the barber from chemical burns and allergic reactions.

quizQuestions.question: A client with scalp abrasions should:
options: Have the abrasion treated and return
quizQuestions.explanation: Scalp abrasions are a contraindication — chemicals applied to broken skin can cause severe burns.

quizQuestions.question: Where is a patch test typically applied?
options: Behind the ear or inside the elbow
quizQuestions.explanation: Behind the ear or inside the elbow are standard patch test locations — sensitive but discreet.

quizQuestions.question: Signs of a positive (adverse) reaction to a patch test include:
quizQuestions.explanation: Redness, swelling, or itching at the patch test site indicate an allergic reaction — do not proceed.

lessons.title: Relaxers & Texturizers
lessons.objective: Understand relaxer chemistry and application safety.
lessons.content:
Relaxers and Texturizers

How Relaxers Work

Relaxers break the disulfide bonds in the cortex that give hair its curl pattern. The hair is then restructured in a straighter form.

Types

  • Lye relaxers (sodium hydroxide) — faster processing, stronger
  • No-lye relaxers (guanidine) — gentler, less scalp irritation
  • Texturizers — same chemistry, shorter processing time; loosens curl without fully straightening

Application Rules

  • Never apply to a scratched or irritated scalp
  • Base the scalp with petroleum jelly before application
  • Process only to the manufacturer's recommended time
  • Neutralize thoroughly — stops the chemical process

quizQuestions.question: What do relaxers break in the hair to achieve straightening?
quizQuestions.explanation: Relaxers break the disulfide bonds in the cortex — the bonds that give hair its curl pattern.

quizQuestions.question: Lye relaxers contain which active ingredient?
quizQuestions.explanation: Lye relaxers use sodium hydroxide (NaOH) as the active straightening agent.

quizQuestions.question: Before applying a relaxer, the scalp should be protected with:
quizQuestions.explanation: Petroleum jelly (base) protects the scalp from chemical burns during relaxer application.

quizQuestions.question: How does a texturizer differ from a full relaxer?
options: It uses a different active chemical
It has a shorter processing time and only loosens the curl
It is applied with a brush only
quizQuestions.explanation: A texturizer uses the same chemistry but is processed for a shorter time, loosening the curl pattern without fully straightening.

quizQuestions.question: What stops the relaxer chemical process?
quizQuestions.explanation: The neutralizer restores the hair's pH and stops the relaxer from processing further.

lessons.title: Scalp Treatments
lessons.objective: Select and apply appropriate scalp treatments for common conditions.
lessons.content:
Scalp Treatments

Types of Treatments

  • Moisturizing treatment — for dry, flaky scalp
  • Clarifying treatment — removes product buildup
  • Anti-dandruff treatment — contains zinc pyrithione or selenium sulfide
  • Stimulating treatment — increases circulation; contains menthol or peppermint

Application

  1. Shampoo hair first
  2. Apply treatment directly to scalp in sections
  3. Massage in with fingertips
  4. Process per manufacturer instructions
  5. Rinse thoroughly

quizQuestions.question: Which scalp treatment is used to remove product buildup?
quizQuestions.explanation: A clarifying treatment uses chelating agents to remove mineral and product buildup from the scalp and hair.

quizQuestions.question: Anti-dandruff shampoos and treatments contain which active ingredient?
quizQuestions.explanation: Zinc pyrithione and selenium sulfide are antifungal agents that control the Malassezia yeast causing dandruff.

quizQuestions.question: What is the FIRST step before applying a scalp treatment?
quizQuestions.explanation: Shampooing first removes oils and buildup so the treatment can penetrate the scalp effectively.

quizQuestions.question: The tingling sensation in stimulating scalp treatments comes from:
quizQuestions.explanation: Menthol and peppermint oil create a cooling/tingling sensation that also promotes circulation.

quizQuestions.question: Where should a scalp treatment be applied?
options: In sections, directly to the scalp
Mixed with shampoo and applied all over
quizQuestions.explanation: Section-by-section application to the scalp ensures even coverage and direct contact with the target area.

lessons.title: Chemical Services Checkpoint
lessons.objective: Demonstrate mastery of chemical service knowledge.
lessons.content:
Module 6 Review — Chemical Services

Review before taking this checkpoint: color wheel and hair color levels, types of hair color, patch testing, chemical safety PPE, relaxer chemistry and application rules, and scalp treatments. Score 70% or higher to advance.

quizQuestions.question: How long before a chemical service should a patch test be performed?
quizQuestions.explanation: A patch test needs 24-48 hours to reveal any allergic reaction.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of hair color requires a developer to open the cuticle?
quizQuestions.explanation: Permanent color uses developer (hydrogen peroxide) to open the cuticle and deposit color.

quizQuestions.question: What stops the chemical process during a relaxer service?
quizQuestions.explanation: The neutralizer restores the hair's pH and stops the relaxer from processing.

quizQuestions.question: Hair color level 1 represents:
quizQuestions.explanation: Level 1 is the darkest — black. Level 10 is the lightest blonde.

quizQuestions.question: Before applying a relaxer, the scalp should be:
quizQuestions.explanation: Petroleum jelly protects the scalp from chemical burns during relaxer application.

lessons.title: Building Your Clientele
lessons.objective: Apply strategies to attract and retain clients.
lessons.content:
Building Your Clientele

First Impressions

Clients decide within the first 30 seconds whether they will return. Be on time, be clean, be professional.

Retention Strategies

  • Remember client names and preferences
  • Keep a client card with notes on their style, products used, and last visit
  • Follow up after new clients — a simple text goes a long way
  • Recommend rebooking before they leave the chair

Social Media

Post your work consistently. Before-and-after photos with client permission are the most effective content. Use local hashtags and tag your shop location.

quizQuestions.question: Within how many seconds do clients typically decide whether they will return?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clients form a first impression within the first 30 seconds — punctuality, cleanliness, and professionalism matter immediately.

quizQuestions.question: What is a client card used for?
options: Storing notes on style, products used, and last visit
quizQuestions.explanation: A client card holds notes on the client's style preferences, products used, and past visits — essential for building rapport.

quizQuestions.question: When is the best time to recommend rebooking to a client?
quizQuestions.explanation: Recommending the next appointment before the client leaves is the most effective retention strategy.

quizQuestions.question: The most effective social media content for barbers is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Before-and-after photos directly showcase your skill and are the most compelling content for attracting new clients.

quizQuestions.question: How should a new client be followed up with after their first visit?
options: A simple text or call goes a long way
Wait for them to book again on their own
quizQuestions.explanation: A brief follow-up after the first visit shows care and significantly increases the chance of return.

lessons.title: Booth Rental vs. Commission vs. Ownership
lessons.objective: Compare barbershop business models and their financial implications.
lessons.content:
Barbershop Business Models

Commission

You work for the shop owner and receive a percentage of your service revenue (typically 40-60%). The shop provides clients, supplies, and equipment. Good for new barbers building skills.

Booth Rental

You pay the shop owner a weekly or monthly fee to use a chair. You keep 100% of your service revenue. You are self-employed — responsible for your own taxes, supplies, and clients.

Shop Ownership

You own the business. Maximum income potential but maximum responsibility. Requires business license, shop license, and significant startup capital.

Which is Right for You?

Most barbers start on commission, move to booth rental as they build clientele, and consider ownership after 5+ years of experience.

quizQuestions.question: In a commission arrangement, the barber typically receives what percentage of service revenue?
quizQuestions.explanation: Commission barbers typically receive 40–60% of service revenue; the shop provides clients, supplies, and equipment.

quizQuestions.question: In a booth rental arrangement, who is responsible for paying their own taxes?
quizQuestions.explanation: Booth renters are independent contractors — they are self-employed and responsible for all their own taxes.

quizQuestions.question: Which business model offers the maximum income potential?
quizQuestions.explanation: Shop ownership has the highest income ceiling but also carries the most financial risk and responsibility.

quizQuestions.question: Which model is most recommended for a new barber just starting out?
quizQuestions.explanation: Commission is best for beginners — the shop provides clients and support while the barber builds skills and clientele.

quizQuestions.question: A booth renter pays the shop owner:
options: A fixed weekly or monthly booth fee
quizQuestions.explanation: Booth renters pay a fixed fee (weekly or monthly) for the use of their chair — not a percentage of earnings.

lessons.title: Pricing, Tipping & Financial Basics
lessons.objective: Set competitive prices and manage basic barbershop finances.
lessons.content:
Pricing and Finances

Setting Your Prices

  • Research local market rates
  • Factor in your experience level
  • Price for the service, not the time
  • Raise prices as your clientele grows — do not undervalue your work

Tipping

The standard tip for barbering is 15-20%. Never expect a tip but always appreciate one. Make it easy — have a tip jar or use a payment system that prompts for tips.

Taxes as a Self-Employed Barber

  • Track all income — cash and card
  • Set aside 25-30% for taxes
  • Keep receipts for all business expenses (supplies, tools, education)
  • Pay quarterly estimated taxes to avoid penalties

quizQuestions.question: What percentage of income should a self-employed barber set aside for taxes?
quizQuestions.explanation: Self-employed barbers pay both income tax and self-employment tax (Social Security + Medicare), totaling 25–30%.

quizQuestions.question: The standard tip for barbering services is:
options: Tips are not standard for barbers
quizQuestions.explanation: 15–20% is the industry standard tip for personal service professionals including barbers.

quizQuestions.question: Why should a barber pay quarterly estimated taxes?
options: It reduces the total tax owed
It is required by Indiana state law
quizQuestions.explanation: Self-employed individuals must pay estimated taxes quarterly or face underpayment penalties at tax filing time.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a deductible business expense for a barber?
quizQuestions.explanation: Professional tools, supplies, education, and booth rental fees are all legitimate deductible business expenses.

quizQuestions.question: How should prices be set as a new barber?
options: Based on local market rates and experience level
quizQuestions.explanation: Research local market rates and factor in your experience level — raise prices as your clientele and skills grow.

lessons.title: Professionalism & Ethics
lessons.objective: Apply professional and ethical standards in the barbershop.
lessons.content:
Professionalism and Ethics

The Barber's Code

  • Never speak negatively about other barbers or shops
  • Respect client confidentiality — what happens in the chair stays in the chair
  • Do not perform services outside your scope of practice
  • Be honest about what you can and cannot achieve

Handling Difficult Clients

Stay calm. Listen. Offer to fix the issue at no charge if it is your error. If a client is abusive, you have the right to refuse service.

Continuing Education

The barbering industry evolves constantly. Attend trade shows, watch tutorials, and practice new techniques. Indiana requires continuing education for license renewal.

quizQuestions.question: The barber's code says you should never:
options: Speak negatively about other barbers or shops
quizQuestions.explanation: Speaking negatively about competitors is unprofessional, harms the industry's reputation, and reflects poorly on you.

quizQuestions.question: If a client complains that their haircut is wrong, you should:
options: Argue that the cut is correct
Offer to fix it at no charge if it is your error
Ask them to return another day
quizQuestions.explanation: Taking ownership of mistakes and offering a free correction maintains client trust and professionalism.

quizQuestions.question: Client information shared during a service should be:
options: Shared with other barbers at the shop
quizQuestions.explanation: Client confidentiality is a professional obligation — what is shared in the chair stays in the chair.

quizQuestions.question: Indiana requires what for barber license renewal?
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana mandates continuing education as a condition of renewing a barber license every two years.

quizQuestions.question: If a client becomes verbally abusive, a barber:
options: Has the right to refuse service
Should call the state board immediately
quizQuestions.explanation: Barbers have the right to refuse service to abusive or disruptive clients to maintain a safe workplace.

lessons.title: Styling Products & Finishing
lessons.objective: Select and apply appropriate styling products for different hair types.
lessons.content:
Styling Products

Product Types

  • Pomade — medium to high hold, medium to high shine; classic barbershop finish
  • Clay — medium to high hold, matte finish; modern styles
  • Cream — light hold, natural finish; good for textured hair
  • Gel — strong hold, high shine; waves and slick styles
  • Wax — flexible hold; mustaches and detailed styling

Application

  1. Start with a small amount — you can always add more
  2. Warm product between palms
  3. Work through hair evenly
  4. Style with comb or fingers

quizQuestions.question: Which styling product provides high hold with a MATTE finish?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clay provides medium-to-high hold with a natural matte finish — popular for modern textured styles.

quizQuestions.question: Pomade is best described as providing:
options: Light hold with a matte finish
Light hold with a natural finish
quizQuestions.explanation: Pomade provides medium-to-high hold with a glossy, high-shine finish — classic for barbershop styles.

quizQuestions.question: Gel is the best choice for:
quizQuestions.explanation: Gel provides strong hold and high shine, making it ideal for 360 waves and slick-back styles.

quizQuestions.question: How much product should you start with when styling?
options: A large amount for maximum hold
A small amount — you can always add more
Enough to coat all the hair at once
The same amount for every client regardless of hair type
quizQuestions.explanation: Always start small — over-application is difficult to correct and can make hair look greasy.

quizQuestions.question: Wax is most appropriate for styling:
quizQuestions.explanation: Wax provides flexible hold ideal for mustache styling, spit curls, and other detail work.

lessons.title: Professional Skills Checkpoint
lessons.objective: Demonstrate mastery of professional and business skills.
lessons.content:
Module 7 Review — Professional & Business Skills

Review before taking this checkpoint: client retention strategies, booth rental vs. commission vs. ownership, pricing and taxes, professional ethics, and styling products. Score 70% or higher to advance.

quizQuestions.question: In a booth rental arrangement, who keeps 100% of service revenue?
quizQuestions.explanation: Booth renters are self-employed and keep all service revenue after paying their booth fee.

quizQuestions.question: What percentage of income should a self-employed barber set aside for taxes?
quizQuestions.explanation: Self-employed individuals pay both income tax and self-employment tax, totaling 25-30%.

quizQuestions.question: Which styling product provides high hold with a matte finish?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clay provides medium to high hold with a matte finish, popular for modern styles.

quizQuestions.question: The standard tip for barbering services is:
quizQuestions.explanation: 15-20% is the standard tip for personal service professionals including barbers.

quizQuestions.question: What is the most effective social media content for barbers?
quizQuestions.explanation: Before-and-after photos showcase your skill directly and attract new clients.

lessons.title: Indiana State Board Exam Overview
lessons.objective: Understand the format and requirements of the Indiana barber state board exam.
lessons.content:
Indiana State Board Exam

Exam Components

  • Written exam — 100 multiple choice questions; 75% passing score required
  • Practical exam — performed on a mannequin or live model; graded by state board examiners

Written Exam Topics

  • Infection control and sanitation (25%)
  • Hair science and scalp analysis (20%)
  • Haircutting and styling (25%)
  • Chemical services (15%)
  • Indiana laws and regulations (15%)

Practical Exam Skills

  • Haircut with fade
  • Shave service
  • Sanitation procedures
  • Client draping and preparation

quizQuestions.question: How many questions are on the Indiana barber written exam?
quizQuestions.explanation: The Indiana barber written exam consists of 100 multiple-choice questions.

quizQuestions.question: What passing score is required on the Indiana barber written exam?
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana requires a 75% passing score on the written state board examination.

quizQuestions.question: The Indiana barber practical exam is performed on:
quizQuestions.explanation: The practical exam is evaluated by state board examiners on either a mannequin or live model.

quizQuestions.question: What percentage of the Indiana written exam covers infection control and sanitation?
quizQuestions.explanation: Infection control and sanitation is the largest single category, making up 25% of the written exam.

quizQuestions.question: Indiana laws and regulations account for what percentage of the written exam?
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana laws and regulations comprise 15% of the written exam — the same as chemical services.

lessons.title: Written Exam Review — Sanitation & Science
lessons.objective: Review key concepts in sanitation and hair science for the written exam.
lessons.content:
Written Exam Review: Sanitation & Science

Key Sanitation Facts

  • Disinfection is required between every client — not sterilization
  • EPA-registered disinfectants must be used
  • Sharps go in puncture-resistant containers
  • Disinfectant solution changed daily or when contaminated
  • Tinea capitis = no service, refer to physician

Key Hair Science Facts

  • Cortex contains melanin
  • Anagen = growth phase (2-7 years)
  • Normal hair loss = 50-100 hairs/day
  • High porosity = damaged cuticle
  • Patch test = 24-48 hours before chemical services

quizQuestions.question: When is disinfection required in an Indiana barbershop?
quizQuestions.explanation: EPA-registered disinfection of all tools is required between every client under Indiana state board rules.

quizQuestions.question: Which layer of the hair shaft contains melanin?
quizQuestions.explanation: The cortex is the middle layer of the hair shaft and contains melanin granules that determine hair color.

quizQuestions.question: What is the normal range of daily hair loss for a healthy adult?
quizQuestions.explanation: Losing 50–100 hairs per day is considered within the normal range for healthy hair cycling.

quizQuestions.question: High hair porosity indicates:
quizQuestions.explanation: High porosity results from a damaged or raised cuticle that absorbs moisture quickly but cannot retain it.

quizQuestions.question: The anagen phase of the hair growth cycle lasts approximately:
quizQuestions.explanation: Anagen is the active growth phase, lasting 2–7 years — its length determines maximum hair length.

lessons.title: Written Exam Review — Techniques & Laws
lessons.objective: Review haircutting techniques and Indiana laws for the written exam.
lessons.content:
Written Exam Review: Techniques & Laws

Key Technique Facts

  • Parietal ridge = widest part of head = high fade reference
  • Occipital bone = back of skull = low/mid fade reference
  • Razor angle = 30 degrees
  • Neckline = 2 finger-widths above Adam's apple
  • First shave pass = with the grain
  • Thinning shears = remove bulk, not length

Key Indiana Law Facts

  • Apprenticeship path = 2,000 OJT hours
  • School path = 1,500 hours
  • Written exam passing score = 75%
  • License renewal = every 2 years
  • License must be displayed at workstation
  • Governed by Indiana Code Title 25, Article 8

quizQuestions.question: How many hours does the Indiana barber school path require?
quizQuestions.explanation: The cosmetology/barber school path requires 1,500 hours in Indiana.

quizQuestions.question: How many OJT hours does the Indiana apprenticeship path require?
quizQuestions.explanation: The DOL-registered apprenticeship path requires 2,000 on-the-job training hours.

quizQuestions.question: The Indiana Barber Act is governed by which section of state law?
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana Code Title 25, Article 8 governs the licensing and practice of barbering in Indiana.

quizQuestions.question: Indiana barber licenses must be renewed every:
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana requires barber license renewal every two years.

quizQuestions.question: The parietal ridge is the reference point for which type of fade?
quizQuestions.explanation: The parietal ridge — the widest part of the head — is the reference point for starting a high fade.

lessons.title: Practical Exam Preparation
lessons.objective: Prepare for the practical exam with a structured practice checklist.
lessons.content:
Practical Exam Preparation

What Examiners Look For

  • Proper draping and client preparation
  • Sanitation procedures performed correctly
  • Clean, even fade with smooth transitions
  • Sharp lineup
  • Proper razor technique and safety
  • Professional demeanor throughout

Practice Checklist

  1. Drape client correctly with neck strip and cape
  2. Disinfect all tools before beginning
  3. Establish fade line and work upward
  4. Blend all transitions — no lines
  5. Execute clean lineup at hairline, temples, and nape
  6. Perform shave with correct angle and grain direction
  7. Apply post-shave care
  8. Clean and disinfect station after service

quizQuestions.question: What is placed around the client's neck BEFORE the cutting cape?
options: Nothing — the cape goes directly on skin
quizQuestions.explanation: A neck strip (or paper neck strip) prevents the cape from directly touching and irritating the client's skin.

quizQuestions.question: During the practical exam, examiners look for which of the following FIRST?
quizQuestions.explanation: State board examiners evaluate proper draping and sanitation procedures as the first and foundational element of the practical exam.

quizQuestions.question: What must be done immediately after finishing a service during the practical exam?
options: Leave tools for the next candidate
Clean and disinfect tools and workstation
Style the client's hair with product
Remove the cape and dismiss the client
quizQuestions.explanation: Post-service cleanup and disinfection of tools and workstation is a scored element of the practical exam.

quizQuestions.question: The razor angle for the shave portion of the practical exam is:
quizQuestions.explanation: A 30-degree razor angle is the standard taught and evaluated on the Indiana state board practical exam.

quizQuestions.question: During the practical exam, which fade is most commonly tested?
options: Haircut with fade — any type executed cleanly
quizQuestions.explanation: The practical exam typically requires a haircut with a fade, and examiners evaluate the quality and blending regardless of fade type.

lessons.title: Program Final Exam
lessons.objective: Demonstrate comprehensive mastery of the barber apprenticeship curriculum.
lessons.content:
Program Final Exam

This comprehensive final exam covers all modules of the Indiana Barber Apprenticeship program. Topics include infection control and sanitation, hair science, tools and equipment, haircutting techniques, shaving and beard services, chemical services, professional skills, anatomy and physiology, skin science, shampooing and conditioning, thermal styling, advanced color, Indiana laws, and career development. Score 70% or higher to complete the program and be eligible for the Indiana state board exams.

quizQuestions.question: What type of disinfectant is required in Indiana barbershops?
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana requires EPA-registered disinfectants — these meet federal efficacy standards for killing pathogens on tools.

quizQuestions.question: Disinfectant solution in a wet sanitizer should be changed:
quizQuestions.explanation: Disinfectant solution must be changed daily or whenever it becomes contaminated or visibly soiled.

quizQuestions.question: A client has tinea capitis. You should:
options: Decline service and refer to a physician
quizQuestions.explanation: Tinea capitis (ringworm of the scalp) is a contagious fungal infection — no services may be performed.

quizQuestions.question: Shavette blades must be disposed of in:
quizQuestions.explanation: Single-use sharps are biohazardous and must always go into a puncture-resistant sharps container.

quizQuestions.question: When blood exposure occurs during a service, what is the FIRST action?
options: Apply antiseptic to the barber's hands
quizQuestions.explanation: Stopping the service immediately is the first step in the blood exposure protocol — client safety comes first.

quizQuestions.question: Which layer of the hair shaft contains melanin?
quizQuestions.explanation: The cortex is the middle layer of the hair shaft and contains the melanin granules that determine hair color.

quizQuestions.question: The active hair growth phase is called:
quizQuestions.explanation: Anagen is the active growth phase, lasting 2–7 years and determining the potential length of the hair.

quizQuestions.question: Normal daily hair loss for a healthy adult is approximately:
quizQuestions.explanation: Losing 50–100 hairs per day is within the normal range for the hair growth cycle.

quizQuestions.question: High porosity hair indicates:
quizQuestions.explanation: High porosity results from a damaged or raised cuticle that absorbs moisture quickly but cannot retain it.

quizQuestions.question: The healthy pH range for hair and scalp is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Hair and scalp are slightly acidic at pH 4.5–5.5. Products outside this range can disrupt the moisture balance.

quizQuestions.question: What is placed around the client's neck before the cutting cape?
quizQuestions.explanation: A neck strip (paper neck strip) prevents the cape from directly touching and irritating the client's skin.

quizQuestions.question: How many drops of oil should be applied to clipper blades during maintenance?
quizQuestions.explanation: 2–3 drops while the clipper is running is sufficient — excess oil attracts hair debris.

quizQuestions.question: The #0 clipper guard (no guard attached) produces:
quizQuestions.explanation: Removing the guard entirely (or using #0) produces the closest possible clipper cut — used for skin fades.

quizQuestions.question: Thinning shears are used to:
options: Remove bulk without changing overall length
quizQuestions.explanation: Thinning shears have one serrated blade and are designed to remove bulk while maintaining length.

quizQuestions.question: The correct chair height for ergonomic barbering positions the barber to work at:
quizQuestions.explanation: Working at elbow level keeps the back straight, prevents hunching, and reduces repetitive strain.

quizQuestions.question: The neckline is set at:
options: Two finger-widths above the Adam's apple
quizQuestions.explanation: Two finger-widths above the Adam's apple is the standard neckline position taught in state board preparation.

quizQuestions.question: A mid fade starts at which reference point?
quizQuestions.explanation: A mid fade begins at the temple area — between the low fade (near occipital) and high fade (parietal ridge).

quizQuestions.question: The parietal ridge is the reference point for:
quizQuestions.explanation: The parietal ridge — the widest part of the head — is the starting point for a high fade.

quizQuestions.question: Point cutting is used to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Point cutting removes weight from the ends and adds texture and movement to the hair.

quizQuestions.question: Scissor over comb produces a result that is:
options: Softer and more natural than clipper over comb
Always shorter than clipper over comb
quizQuestions.explanation: Scissor over comb produces a softer, more natural finish, often preferred for textured or ethnic hair.

quizQuestions.question: The first pass in a three-pass straight razor shave goes:
quizQuestions.explanation: The first pass (WTG — with the grain) safely removes the bulk of the beard before closer passes.

quizQuestions.question: The straight razor should be held at what angle to the skin?
quizQuestions.explanation: A 30-degree angle provides optimal closeness while minimizing the risk of cuts.

quizQuestions.question: Razor bumps (pseudofolliculitis barbae) are most common in clients with:
quizQuestions.explanation: Curly hair tends to re-enter the skin after shaving, causing ingrown hairs and razor bumps.

quizQuestions.question: Which product is applied to a razor nick to stop minor bleeding?
quizQuestions.explanation: An alum block is an antiseptic astringent that constricts blood vessels to stop minor bleeding from nicks.

quizQuestions.question: The standard neckline position for beard design is:
options: Two finger-widths above the Adam's apple
quizQuestions.explanation: Two finger-widths above the Adam's apple is the industry standard for beard necklines.

quizQuestions.question: A patch test must be performed how long before a chemical service?
quizQuestions.explanation: A 24–48 hour window is required to detect any allergic reaction before performing the service.

quizQuestions.question: Relaxers break which bonds in the hair to straighten it?
quizQuestions.explanation: Chemical relaxers break the disulfide bonds in the hair cortex — the bonds that create the curl pattern.

quizQuestions.question: What stops the relaxer chemical process?
quizQuestions.explanation: The neutralizer restores the hair's pH and halts the relaxer from processing further.

quizQuestions.question: Hair color level 1 represents:
quizQuestions.explanation: Level 1 is the darkest (black). Level 10 is the lightest (pale blonde). The scale goes darkest to lightest.

quizQuestions.question: Before applying a relaxer, the scalp should be protected with:
quizQuestions.explanation: Petroleum jelly (base) is applied to the scalp to protect it from chemical burns during relaxer application.

quizQuestions.question: Indiana barber licenses must be renewed every:
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana requires barber license renewal every two years.

quizQuestions.question: In a booth rental arrangement, the barber keeps what percentage of service revenue?
quizQuestions.explanation: Booth renters are self-employed — they keep 100% of service revenue after paying their fixed booth fee.

quizQuestions.question: What is the standard tip for barbering services?
quizQuestions.explanation: 15–20% is the industry standard tip for personal service professionals including barbers.

quizQuestions.question: Self-employed barbers should set aside what percentage of income for taxes?
quizQuestions.explanation: Self-employed individuals pay income tax plus self-employment tax (Social Security + Medicare), totaling 25–30%.

quizQuestions.question: Which styling product provides medium-to-high hold with a MATTE finish?
quizQuestions.explanation: Clay provides medium-to-high hold with a natural matte finish — popular for modern textured styles.

quizQuestions.question: The Indiana Barber Act is located in:
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana Code Title 25, Article 8 governs all licensed barbers and barbershops in Indiana.

quizQuestions.question: Barbershop licenses in Indiana are issued by:
options: The Indiana State Board of Education
quizQuestions.explanation: The Indiana Professional Licensing Agency (IPLA) administers all barber and barbershop licenses in the state.

quizQuestions.question: Indiana barber licenses must be displayed:
options: In the shop owner's office only
quizQuestions.explanation: Each licensed barber's license must be displayed at their workstation — this is an inspectable requirement.

quizQuestions.question: The passing score for the Indiana barber written state board exam is:
quizQuestions.explanation: Indiana requires a 75% passing score on the written portion of the state board examination.

quizQuestions.question: The DOL-registered apprenticeship path to a barber license in Indiana requires:
quizQuestions.explanation: The Department of Labor registered apprenticeship path requires 2,000 on-the-job training hours.

quizQuestions.question: The human skull has how many cranial bones?
quizQuestions.explanation: There are 8 cranial bones: frontal, 2 parietal, 2 temporal, occipital, ethmoid, and sphenoid.

quizQuestions.question: The largest movable bone in the face is the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The mandible (lower jaw) is the only movable bone of the face — it hinges at the temporomandibular joint.

quizQuestions.question: The muscle primarily responsible for chewing is the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The masseter muscle closes the jaw and is the primary muscle of mastication (chewing).

quizQuestions.question: The trigeminal nerve is cranial nerve number:
quizQuestions.explanation: Cranial nerve V (the trigeminal) provides sensation to the face and scalp and is key to facial massage knowledge.

quizQuestions.question: Sebum is produced by:
quizQuestions.explanation: Sebaceous (oil) glands produce sebum, which lubricates the hair and skin and is part of the acid mantle.

quizQuestions.question: Hair follicles and sebaceous glands are located in the:
quizQuestions.explanation: The dermis (middle layer of the skin) contains hair follicles, sebaceous glands, sweat glands, and blood vessels.

quizQuestions.question: Oily skin is characterized by:
options: Small tight pores and a matte finish
Enlarged pores and a shiny appearance
quizQuestions.explanation: Oily skin results from overactive sebaceous glands — enlarged pores and shininess are the telltale signs.

quizQuestions.question: The opening and closing stroke used in facial massage is called:
quizQuestions.explanation: Effleurage is the light, stroking movement used to open and close a facial massage — it relaxes and prepares the skin.

quizQuestions.question: 20-volume developer lifts hair approximately:
quizQuestions.explanation: 20-volume developer provides 1–2 levels of lift — the most common developer for permanent color services.

quizQuestions.question: Purple toner is used to neutralize which unwanted hair tone?
quizQuestions.explanation: Purple and yellow are complementary colors — purple toner cancels out yellow/brassy tones in lightened hair.
